replayHooks: High-trust path unlocks hidden lease archive ending.
Hard fail states include death, possession, and total case collapse.
Different branch discipline determines whether the villain rises or falls.
sequelHooks: Other cities report rent receipts tied to non-existent units.
branchingMoments: Expose fraud publicly vs close case quietly

caseFile.objective: Resolve The Fourth Tenant and identify occupancy fraud operators and link deaths to lease-chain manipulation.
caseFile.primaryQuestion: Who controls the active manipulation path in tenement service core, and what truth are they preventing from becoming durable record?
caseFile.operationWindow: 72-hour stabilization, then progressive escalation toward final branch lock.
successCriteria: Clear chronology that can be understood and repeated by first-time players.
Witness testimony that remains coherent under adversarial reinterpretation.
Evidence provenance recorded before narrative interpretation.
Final branch chosen with explicit ethical and operational rationale.
Keep villain advantage below critical takeover threshold.
Preserve witness testimony through final debrief.
Maintain admissible chain-of-custody on primary evidence.
failureConsequences: The Landlord of Rooms That Move gains control of the narrative around Flood-district prewar apartment block.
At least one witness or ally is killed, abducted, or mentally broken.
Core impossible apartment records evidence is destroyed or rendered inadmissible.
Public trust collapses and the investigation is politically buried.
Mission failure forces a full restart from Day 1.
